2606.24627.title: The Warrant Gap: Claim-Conditioned Re-scoring for Fact-Checking
2606.24627.paper_summary: Fact-checking systems built on LLMs achieve high verdict accuracy on standard benchmarks, yet routinely output Supports labels whose cited evidence does not license the claim. Structured decomposition is the natural way to inspect those warrants, but rigid extraction protocols strip the full-claim context that facets need. We introduce SIFT -- claim-conditioned re-scoring of extracted evidence spans against the full claim -- paired with WSP (Warranted Supports Proportion), an automatic NLI check that the cited warrant entails the claim. We evaluate on FEVER, SciFact, 5PILS, and DP across four open-source backbones. SIFT recovers accuracy on cells where naive decomposition costs up to 27.6 points, while raising WSP above direct prompting; WSP itself calibrates against human gold evidence at AUC 0.92 and precision 0.98.
2606.24627.paper_summary_zh: 事實查核系統基於大型語言模型（LLMs）在標準基準上達到了高判決準確率，但經常輸出其引用證據無法支持的支持標籤。結構化分解是檢查這些保證的自然方式，但僵化的提取協議剝奪了面向所需的完整主張上下文。我們介紹了SIFT——對提取的證據範圍進行主張條件的重新評分，並與WSP（保證支持比例）配對，這是一種自動的自然語言推理檢查，確保所引用的保證涵蓋了主張。我們在FEVER、SciFact、5PILS和DP上進行評估，涵蓋四個開源骨幹。SIFT在天真分解損失高達27.6分的情況下恢復了準確性，同時將WSP提升到超過直接提示的水平；WSP本身在AUC 0.92和精確度0.98的情況下對人類金證據進行了校準。
2606.24627.author: Arka Ujjal Dey et.al.
